name: Run seed migration (production + staging Sanity datasets)
description: Seed the 4 not-yet-Sanity-wired email templates + emailPrivacyExport doc-types so editor surfaces have content on first publish.
result: Staging seed run idempotent. Created 3 new docs on staging Sanity: emailGiftPurchaseConfirmation, emailRecipientIntakeReceived, emailPrivacyExport. Production seed deferred to PR-merge time (out of this session's scope). Smoke verified all 8 templates render 200 with seeded copy.

name: Worker-bundle sourcemap upload to Sentry (BLOCKED upstream)
description: Blocked on sentry-javascript#19213. sentry-cli sourcemaps inject/upload succeeds against .open-next/worker.js[.map] but runtime stack frames don't reference uploaded Debug IDs because something in OpenNext's worker init strips them. Server-side stack traces from Sentry.withSentry reach Sentry minified. Acceptable tradeoff — error.digest provides per-error correlation; client-side captures are symbolicated.

name: Apply production D1 migrations 0004 → 0012
description: Staging D1 is at 0012 (verified 2026-05-19); production stuck at 0003. 9 migrations introduce every gift-aware column (is_gift, purchaser_user_id, recipient_email, gift_delivery_method, gift_send_at, gift_message, gift_claim_token_hash, gift_claim_email_fired_at, gift_claimed_at, gift_cancelled_at, gift_resend_lock_until) + 0012's 5 audit columns + UNIQUE partial idx (gift_claim_sent_now_at, gift_claim_sent_now_actor, gift_claim_prior_alarm_at, gift_cancelled_by, gift_cancelled_reason, idx_gift_sent_now_once). If main-merge happens without applying, production worker throws at first gift-aware code path. Order: (1) pnpm migrate:list:staging confirm 0012, (2) pnpm migrate:apply:prod (CLI applies pending in lexicographic order), (3) pnpm migrate:list:prod confirm all 12, (4) wrangler d1 execute withjosephine_bookings --remote --command "PRAGMA table_info(submissions);" spot-check. 0012 alone (idempotent ALTER + CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS) is safe standalone.

name: Evaluate cross-client email testing tool (Litmus / Email-On-Acid / Resend preview)
description:
PLANNING / EVALUATION subtask. Locked 2026-05-24 by Max ("plan for it now" vs "defer until render bug").

WHY NEEDED
React Email + iframed preview shows Chrome rendering, not Gmail / Outlook / Apple Mail. Email clients strip CSS aggressively (no flexbox-gap, limited @media, no CSS custom properties without fallbacks in most). The iframe-pane preview catches edit-time copy/layout but NOT client-specific rendering bugs. Send-to-test (separate subtask) gives accurate Gmail rendering for Becky's inbox but doesn't cover Outlook desktop / Apple Mail dark mode / Yahoo / etc.

DELIVERABLE
Written eval report at www/MEMORY/WORK/<slug>/CROSS_CLIENT_EVAL.md comparing:

1. Litmus (litmus.com) — industry standard. Pricing tiers (free trial → Basic $99/mo → higher tiers). API + visual diff. 90+ client previews.
2. Email-On-Acid (emailonacid.com) — direct competitor to Litmus. Pricing. Coverage.
3. Resend's built-in preview — currently none in the dashboard; check 2026 state. Free if it exists.
4. Mailchimp's Inbox Inspector (if standalone access still exists).
5. HTMLemail.io / Email Comb minifiers — preprocessing tools that strip non-compatible CSS automatically; cheaper than visual tests, prevents some bugs before they ship.

DECISION CRITERIA
- Coverage breadth (how many clients tested per send).
- Cost vs project scale (~10 customers/month at launch).
- Integration shape: manual upload of HTML vs API webhook vs no-integration-needed (visual-diff a deployed-static-render).
- Whether dark-mode rendering is covered (Outlook + Apple Mail diverge here).

OUTPUT
Recommendation: adopt one + monthly budget, OR defer with explicit trigger (e.g., first customer reports render bug in client X). Then file the follow-up tasks if adopted.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Actual integration (separate subtask if eval recommends adoption).
- Free-tool patchwork as a substitute (e.g., manually opening each email in each client) — that's labor-intensive and doesn't scale; the eval is about tool-buy decision.

TRIGGER FOR EVAL: any time after the iframe-pane swap + send-to-test subtasks land. NOT blocking on launch.

name: Research — Storybook 9.x for Next.js App Router + Tailwind v4 + Sanity + MCPs
description:
Deliverable: written research report at www/MEMORY/WORK/<slug>/RESEARCH.md with citations to vendor docs, blog posts, working examples in other projects. NOT a plan — the plan-discussion subtask reads this and locks decisions afterwards.

Topics:

1. STORYBOOK VERSION + NEXT.JS APP ROUTER + RSC
   - Latest stable Storybook (likely 9.x as of 2026); current LTS posture
   - @storybook/nextjs vs @storybook/experimental-nextjs-vite — which framework integration is recommended for App Router projects in 2026
   - RSC story support — what works, what's flagged experimental, edge cases
   - Server Action mocking, async-component stories, useFormState/useActionState handling

2. TAILWIND v4 + tokens.css WIRING
   - @theme directive in preview-head.html or .storybook/preview.ts
   - HMR for token changes (does editing tokens.css live-update stories?)
   - Compatibility with Storybook's PostCSS pipeline
   - Any known issues with Storybook + Tailwind v4 specifically (v4 is newer than most Storybook docs)

3. SANITY GROQ MOCKING
   - MSW handlers in browser context — workerd-isolate gotcha (CLAUDE.md known-pitfall) DOES NOT apply since Storybook runs in browser. Confirm.
   - Alternative: mock the Sanity client at module level via Storybook decorators or framework parameters
   - Alternative: feed fetched data as Story props (component takes already-fetched data; story bypasses fetch entirely)
   - Pattern: how to express the same page in different states (cold-visit / check-email / rested / DeliveredSurface for /listen) without hand-rolling 4 separate fetch mocks
   - Sanity Live (next-sanity defineLive) interaction with Storybook — does sanityFetch work in non-Next runtime?

4. NEXT.JS ROUTING MOCKING
   - useParams, useSearchParams, usePathname under @storybook/nextjs — supported via parameters.nextjs.navigation
   - notFound() / redirect() — how stories react
   - middleware.ts — does it run in Storybook context (it shouldn't)
   - Cookie-based auth (listen sessions) — best practice for storying cookie-gated states

5. STORYBOOK MCPs (the AI integration angle)
   - Confirm canonical MCP server (Storybook team announced one in 2025 — verify version, repo)
   - What it exposes (list stories, render a story, capture screenshot, get component props/args, etc.)
   - Whether the MCP can be used in Claude Code / this project's tooling
   - Alternative MCPs in the design-system / component-library space (Chromatic MCP? Tailwind MCP?) — quick survey

6. HOSTING / VISUAL REGRESSION
   - Local-only vs static export to Cloudflare Pages (existing CF account) vs Chromatic
   - If Chromatic: cost, free-tier limits, visual diff workflow
   - If static export: how to publish + access-gate (CF Access? Pages auth?)
   - CI integration: should PRs trigger story-build + visual diff? worth it at this project's scale?

7. WORKERD DEV-BINDINGS COMPATIBILITY
   - Does Storybook need access to D1 / R2 / DO bindings? (Should NOT — components consuming fetched data should be storied at the component layer, not via real bindings.)
   - Confirm @opennextjs/cloudflare imports don't error at story-load time
   - If they do: how to mock getCloudflareContext (it's already mocked in vitest per CLAUDE.md DO-bindings rule — pattern is known)

8. BUILD PERF + DEV-LOOP COST
   - How long does Storybook take to boot against this codebase?
   - HMR latency for a single component edit
   - Does running Storybook + pnpm dev simultaneously share build artifacts or duplicate work?

9. ALTERNATIVES (sanity check, not deep-dive)
   - Ladle, Histoire — quick comparison vs Storybook for this stack
   - Plain Next.js routes with mock data toggled via env (cheaper, less feature-rich)
   - Verdict: stay with Storybook unless something concrete disqualifies it

10. PRIOR-ART CHECK
    - Sanity + Next.js + Storybook example repos
    - Any existing patterns we can lift outright

Output: written report, not a plan. Plan-discussion subtask consumes this.
result: Report at www/MEMORY/WORK/20260523-203500_storybook-page-stories-research/RESEARCH.md. Headline: Storybook 10.3.6 + .storybook/ already shipped in repo with 15 component stories. Recommended migration: @storybook/nextjs → @storybook/nextjs-vite. Pages already 80% data-as-props (ListenView verified) — prop-injection stories with zero fetch mocking is the cheapest path. sb.mock pattern translates 1:1 from existing vitest @opennextjs/cloudflare mock. Storybook MCP shipped 10.3 (React-only). 4 open questions flagged for plan-discussion.

name: PR #86 simplify-deferred refactors (3 items)
description: (a) defaults-merge convention across Hero/HowItWorks/Footer/Navigation. (b) Resend EmailSendResult discriminated union. (c) scripts/_lib/sanity-write-client.mts helper extraction.

name: Enhance D-1 — sanity-plugin-iframe-pane swap (load-bearing)
description:
LANDED BEFORE the 4 resume subtasks (seed migration / Pentester audit / smoke / push PR) — those run against the enhanced code, not the as-shipped f6568e9 hand-rolled iframe.

WHAT
1. Add sanity-plugin-iframe-pane@^5 to studio/package.json deps. Current latest verified 5.0.12 (TypeScript types inspected; reload only exposes button?: boolean — npmx changelog claim about reload.revision was wrong).
2. Replace studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx hand-rolled <iframe> with <Iframe> from the plugin.
3. URL resolver bakes in auto-refresh via per-revision query param:
   options: {
     url: (doc) => `${previewOrigin}/api/email-preview/${doc?._type}?rev=${doc?._rev ?? "0"}`,
     attributes: { sandbox: "allow-same-origin" },
     showDisplayUrl: false,
     defaultSize: "desktop",
   }
   Studio re-runs the resolver on every draft save → URL changes → iframe re-mounts → Becky sees the edit live. The worker route ignores ?rev (purely a cache-buster).
4. Worker route /api/email-preview/[type] is unchanged — keep the Origin/Referer allowlist, previewDrafts perspective, SANITY_READ_TOKEN fetch.
5. Fix the silent-fallback when SANITY_READ_TOKEN is missing (flagged in research report Topic 1). Either fail loudly with a 503 + log, or render a clear "preview-token-missing" empty state in the iframe.

OUT OF SCOPE here (separate subtasks): send-to-test doc action, defineLocations additive layer, cross-client tooling eval.

WHY ENHANCE NOT PIVOT (research recommendation, 2026-05-23):
Decision matrix in www/MEMORY/WORK/20260523-211500_phase7-d1-live-preview-research/RESEARCH.md scores enhanced D-1 at 9/10 editor UX vs pivot's 5/10. Pivot to Presentation/defineLocations costs 3-5 days rework with no UX gain. Visual Editing disqualified (wrong shape for <table>-based React Email).

REFERENCES
- Phase 7 PRD: www/MEMORY/WORK/20260517-220000_phase-7-email-sanity-cms/PRD.md
- D-1 research: www/MEMORY/WORK/20260523-211500_phase7-d1-live-preview-research/RESEARCH.md (incl. addendum on reload.revision)
- iframe-pane source: github.com/sanity-io/plugins/tree/main/plugins/sanity-plugin-iframe-pane
- Kevin Green Sanity case study (same architecture as D-1): sanity.io/blog/studio-to-inbox
result: Iframe-pane plugin (5.0.12) swapped in. Cache-bust via ?rev=${_rev||_updatedAt}. SANITY_READ_TOKEN fail-loud (503 + x-preview-reason). emailRecipientIntakeReceived parity (slots + Preview tab + render branch). +104/-26 across 8 files. Quality gates clean (lint, typecheck www+studio, vitest 1676, build www+studio, /simplify 3-agent pass).

name: Dry-run CSV mode
description: Walks submissions WHERE is_gift = 1 AND status = 'paid', flags any with recipient_user_id != purchaser_user_id mismatch chain consistent with the bb5fe157 vector. Emits CSV (submission_id, current recipient_user_id, proposed recipient_user_id, evidence). No writes.

name: a11y audit — time + calendar picker components (TAB nav, focus, ARIA)
description: Verify time picker and date/calendar picker components on intake birth-details page meet a11y compliance. Coverage: (1) keyboard navigation — TAB order is correct, no focus traps, Shift+TAB reverses cleanly; (2) focus visible — focus ring renders on all interactive elements; (3) ARIA — role/labels/aria-expanded/aria-controls on dropdowns and dialog pickers; (4) screen reader — time/date values announced on change; (5) keyboard activation — Enter/Space open pickers, Esc closes, arrow keys move within calendar grid; (6) checkbox 'I don't know my birth time' interaction is keyboard reachable and toggling it correctly enables/disables the time picker without trapping focus. Test surface: src/components/IntakeForm/ birth-details step. Tools: keyboard-only walk, axe-core or Lighthouse a11y audit, VoiceOver/NVDA spot check.

name: Extract SANDBOX_EMAIL_PREFIXES to shared module
description: src/lib/resend.tsx:108 defines sandbox-prefix list as source of truth for dry-run guard. Spec files (gift/listen/stripe/v120-smoke/gift-recipient-listen-roundtrip.spec.ts) each hardcode the matching prefix as raw string. A spec rename OR new spec following slightly different prefix convention silently re-opens the Resend quota leak with zero test failure. Fix: move SANDBOX_EMAIL_PREFIXES + SANDBOX_DOMAIN to new module src/lib/booking/sandboxEmails.ts (worker-safe, no test deps). Import from both src/lib/resend.tsx and spec files; build email addresses as `${SANDBOX_EMAIL_PREFIXES.giftRoundtripPurchaser}${randomUUID()}${SANDBOX_DOMAIN}`. Then typo/rename at any callsite breaks at TypeScript compile.

name: Node 22 → 24 upgrade
description: Pinned in .github/actions/setup/action.yml:13 + .github/workflows/e2e.yml:80 + wrangler compat_date. Target before 2026-10-21 (Node 22 maintenance EOL).

description: Bucket withjosephine-booking-photos → delete objects under submissions/<id>/ for deleted IDs. Or wait for orphan-reaper cron. Sanity asset orphans from listen-roundtrip are LOW priority (deduped by SHA-1; <10KB total).

name: U8 — Claimed-vs-paid status decision
description: Decision needed: add new submission status enum value 'claimed_awaiting_delivery', OR compute claimed-vs-paid from giftClaimedAt presence (no schema churn). Currently the two states are conflated under 'paid'.

Production-only Max-actions to turn vendor calls on. Staging secrets already in place. Run from www/ with the production Cloudflare Workers binding (no --env flag).

description: 27 files, +972/-23. Webhook-secret + admin-token surfaces touched. Standard Phase 7 audit checklist.
result: 6 findings: 1 HIGH + 2 MED + 3 INFO. All closed before push. HIGH F1 fixed via @sanity/preview-url-secret integration (Sanity's documented preview-auth primitives used standalone — see PENTEST_REPORT.md). MEDs F2 (CSP frame-ancestors tightened to STUDIO_ORIGIN_ALLOWLIST) + F3 (503 body generic) fixed inline. Verified live on staging worker 9ef83ed7.

name: Studio iframe — preview smoke test (S.view.component)
description: Slot-validation + preview iframe was the main load-bearing UX piece. Smoke each of the 10 email types: render with placeholders + render with seeded copy.
result: Worker-side smoke complete (all 8 templates 200, origin gate enforces, ?rev transparent, invalid-secret blocks, valid-secret unlocks). Studio-iframe-in-real-Studio visual smoke deferred to Max-verify gate before opening PR.

name: Plan discussion + decisions (after research lands)
description: Reads the research report. Lock decisions across the 10 research topics. Output: implementation subtasks filed under this epic (NOT pre-created — they get shaped by the plan). Decisions to capture: (a) Storybook framework integration choice, (b) Tailwind v4 wiring path, (c) Sanity-mock strategy (MSW / module-mock / prop-injection), (d) routing-mock helpers needed, (e) Storybook MCP — adopt or skip, (f) hosting model (local / static export / Chromatic), (g) workerd-binding mock posture, (h) initial story set (which pages, which states each), (i) any disqualifying findings that flip the decision to an alternative.

name: Mirror repair to Sanity
description: After D1 UPDATE, patch the mirrored Sanity submission doc's recipient.user._ref so /my-gifts queries see the corrected binding.

name: Magic-link email subject + body neutralization or context-routing
description: studio/schemas/emailMagicLink.ts:12,19,33-37 defaults subject + preview + body to reading-centric language. src/app/api/auth/magic-link/route.ts:42-43 fires same template regardless of 'next' destination. A purchaser hitting /my-gifts → 'Send link' gets 'open your reading' wording. Three fix options: (A) neutral copy — edit Sanity defaults to 'Your Josephine sign-in link'. Cheapest, loses warmth. (B) context-aware — thread 'next' (or destination enum) into sendMagicLink, branch copy. (C) per-destination templates — emailMagicLinkListen / emailMagicLinkGiftAccess / emailMagicLinkReadings. Most flexibility, most schema churn. Trigger: UX session before apex unpark OR Becky requests differentiated copy.

name: Reading-price reconcile (Max + Josephine)
description: Pick canonical price per reading; sync Sanity (price cents + priceDisplay) + each Stripe Payment Link. Stripe Prices are immutable: create-new-Price → new-Payment-Link → swap URL on the reading doc → archive old. As of 2026-05-06: soul-blueprint price=17900/display=$129; birth-chart price=9900/display=$89; akashic-record price=7900/display=$89 all fail validation. Consider flipping schema rule warning → error after reconcile so future drift hard-fails Studio save.

name: docs/OPERATIONS.md — un-cancel procedure (D1 console)
description: Document Becky's un-cancel: direct D1 UPDATE setting gift_cancelled_at=NULL + gift_cancelled_by=NULL + gift_cancelled_reason=NULL on the specific submission id. Sanity-webhook→Worker→D1 path deliberately NOT built — single-purchaser-error path doesn't justify the surface.

name: Verify split — round-trip lands on staging only
description: Trigger a staging round-trip spec; confirm checkout.session.completed lands on staging worker. Confirm zero new rows in production Sanity. MUST close before Becky's first real reading.

name: Studio desk restructure — Pages / Emails / Form Building Blocks
description:
Restructure www/studio/schemas/deskStructure.ts into three top-level groups, separating singletons (configured surfaces) from document-type collections (reusable atoms):

📄 Pages (every customer-facing browser surface, comprehensive):
  - Landing, Under Construction, 404 (currently at root)
  - Listen, MyReadings, MyGifts, Magic Link Verify, Gift Claim, Gift Intake (currently in 'Customer emails & pages')
  - 📋 Booking Flow (nested S.list inside Pages):
      - Booking Page, Thank You Page (singletons)
      - Booking Form, Booking Gift Form (singletons — the configured forms)

✉️ Emails (currently interleaved with pages — break out as peer of Pages):
  - emailOrderConfirmation, emailGiftPurchaseConfirmation, emailGiftClaim, emailRecipientIntakeReceived, emailDay2Started, emailDay7Delivery, emailMagicLink, emailPrivacyExport
  - Preserve emailSingletonListItem helper (S.view.component(EmailPreview))

🧩 Form Building Blocks (document-type collections, the reusable atoms):
  - Form Fields (S.documentTypeListItem)
  - Form Sections (S.documentTypeListItem)

Root unchanged: Site Settings, Theme · Readings, Testimonials, FAQ Items · Legal Pages · Submissions

Rationale: Distinguishes singletons (one-doc-per-type configured surfaces) from document-type collections (multi-doc atoms). Pages is comprehensive AND honest (no schema atoms mislabeled as pages). Booking Flow keeps the two form singletons clustered with their Booking Page so the 'new reading type' workflow stays cohesive. Form Fields/Sections rarely accessed directly — Sanity lets editors create them inline via reference picker from inside Booking Form; the Form Building Blocks group is a management view, not primary edit surface.

BLOCKED on mc6p8m8t (Phase 7 email-in-Studio) — that epic owns deskStructure.ts via the EmailPreview wiring; touching the same file now = merge conflict. Start once Phase 7 PR merges.

Risk: zero data risk (singleton documentIds unchanged, only list-item arrangement changes).

name: Split Stripe test-mode webhook off production endpoint
description: Mirror-drift root cause (filed 2026-05-17): prod Stripe test-mode webhook endpoint registered against the prod Stripe account points at https://withjosephine.com/api/stripe/webhook. Round-trip specs trigger real Stripe Checkout, whose checkout.session.completed webhooks land on production worker → writes test data to production Sanity. Surfaced via Phase 5 Bundle A cleanup wiping 59 prod Sanity docs vs 6 actual D1 rows.

name: Send-to-test doc action (env-var recipient allowlist)
description:
ADDITIVE to the iframe-pane swap — gives Becky a real-world cross-client render check by sending the rendered email to a pre-approved inbox.

DECISION (locked 2026-05-24 by Max): recipient allowlist lives as an env var on the Worker (ALLOWED_PREVIEW_RECIPIENTS comma-separated), NOT in Sanity siteSettings. Rationale: Studio compromise can't widen the allowlist; rotating recipients = deploy. Faster + safer security boundary.

WHAT
1. Studio doc-action: studio/actions/sendEmailPreview.ts. Available on each of the 8 email singletons. Title: "Send preview to inbox…". Disabled if doc has unpublished changes (force publish first to preview the published copy; current draft preview already lives in the iframe).
2. Action UI: <Dialog> with a select dropdown of allowlisted recipients (fetched from a new worker endpoint that returns the env-var list — never expose the raw env var to Studio bundle).
3. New worker endpoint: POST /api/admin/send-email-preview. Body: { type, recipient }. Auth: ADMIN_API_KEY header (same pattern as existing studio/actions/resendCustomerEmail.ts). Validates recipient is in ALLOWED_PREVIEW_RECIPIENTS env-var before send. Re-uses src/lib/resend.tsx send functions with the current published Sanity content for that type (NOT defaults — Becky is previewing her edited copy).
4. New worker endpoint: GET /api/admin/list-preview-recipients (auth: ADMIN_API_KEY). Returns the allowlist for the dropdown.
5. Audit row written to deletion_log-equivalent (or a new admin_actions log) per send: { actor: "studio-admin", action: "preview_send", emailType, recipient, sentAt }. Same shape as cascadeDeleteUser audit.
6. Rate-limit: re-use the existing WAF rate-limit on /api/admin/* (rl-josephine-api-write-paths covers it implicitly once the path matches) OR add an in-worker per-recipient cooldown (3 sends per recipient per email-type per 24h, mirroring the existing resend-customer-email cooldown).

PRECEDENT
- studio/actions/resendCustomerEmail.ts (existing — published 2026-05-18 in v1.1.0) is the closest pattern. Lift its shape (auth header, audit write, cooldown).
- See www/MEMORY/WORK/20260517-220000_phase-7-email-sanity-cms/PRD.md for the D-4 origin/referer auth posture (this endpoint can use the stronger ADMIN_API_KEY since it's not iframe-loaded).

DOES NOT BLOCK iframe-pane swap or seed migration — strictly additive. Can ship in PR-A1 if scope allows, OR as a follow-up PR-A3.

name: New BACKLOG items surfaced 2026-05-24

name: U7 + U8 — Studio bundle (preview + claimed-at)
description: Bundle as one Studio-side PR; both touch deskStructure / preview / Presentation wiring.

name: brace-expansion 5.0.5 ReDoS CVE — minimatch upgrade
description: GHSA-jxxr-4gwj-5jf2 filtered in osv-scanner.toml because direct fix breaks toolchain. brace-expansion@5.0.5 MEDIUM ReDoS. Fixed in 5.0.6. Pinning via pnpm.overrides to ^5.0.6 breaks ESLint with TypeError: expand is not a function (minimatch@3.1.5 calling brace-expansion default export as function — broken by 5.0 export-shape change). Promotion path: (1) identify which dep pulls minimatch@3.1.5 (likely @eslint/config-array or vendored eslint plugin). (2) pin minimatch via pnpm.overrides to 9.x+. (3) re-add brace-expansion@<5.0.6: ^5.0.6 override. (4) verify pnpm install + lint + typecheck + test + build clean. (5) remove GHSA entry from osv-scanner.toml. Trigger: (a) CVE escalates MEDIUM → HIGH, (b) brace-expansion reachable from user input at runtime, (c) quarterly toolchain refresh.

name: R2 lifecycle on exports/ prefix (7d expiry)
description: GDPR Art. 20 export ZIPs accumulate without expiry policy. CF Dashboard → R2 → withjosephine-booking-photos → Settings → Object lifecycle rules → Add rule. Rule name: exports-7d-expiry. Prefix: exports/. Action: Delete uploaded objects after 7 Days. Enabled: ✓. Pre-signed download URLs already expire after 7 days; this rule garbage-collects underlying R2 objects so they don't keep counting against storage.

name: Cleanup staging test rows (Variant A/D residue)
description: Submission 67b20b5e-5d91-4dd9-9530-f902f7b3d827 used for Variant A/D walks 2026-05-24 (recipient mgertzen2@gmail.com, purchaser maxgertzen+variantA-purchaser). D1 was patched with bb5fe157's voice/pdf URLs for DeliveredSurface verification. Sweep with PREFIX="maxgertzen+variant" + any +variantC- rows. Same one-liner as the Epic 1 pre-prod cleanup but scoped to staging.

name: C5 — /api/booking/gift-redeem intermittent 400 root cause
description: Sub-PR γ (#163) added structured logging at every silent 400 branch. Next reproduction self-identifies which branch fired. Until then, a fix is speculation. Trigger: next 400 in wrangler tail emits a [gift-redeem] *_rejected log line, OR Max reports another mid-flow submit failure.

name: U5 — Gift-purchase form: prefill purchaser email
description: Gift purchase doesn't prefill purchaser email for repeat customers; standard purchase does. Lift the email-prefill mechanism from self-purchase entry into gift entry page. Quick-win sub-PR candidate alongside U3.

name: PR-D — D-11 cancel-scheduled
description: Per locked D-11 decision: POST /api/gifts/[id]/cancel-scheduled. Same auth pattern as send-now. Ordering: cancelGiftAlarm() FIRST → D1 UPDATE WHERE-guard → rowcount=0 → 409. UPDATE sets gift_cancelled_at, gift_cancelled_by, gift_cancelled_reason='purchaser-request'. CancelScheduledControl (5s confirm-armed, j-rose tint per Quiet Archivist — no alarm-red). Copy explicitly says 'non-refundable' per the wholesale-non-refundable policy locked in PR-B. 'cancelled' pill renders on card after success. docs/OPERATIONS.md updated with Becky's un-cancel procedure (D1 console only — Sanity-webhook→Worker→D1 path not built). Audit columns already exist (migration 0007 + 0012 — no D1 migration). Unblocked since PR-C-ii (PR #147) shipped.

name: U8 — Delivery countdown UI ("5 days left to deliver")
description: Countdown widget on the Submissions list/preview computed from giftClaimedAt + 7-day target.

name: Phase 3 scheduling — PR-D + PR-E (remaining sub-PRs)

Worth re-checking in 6 months; if resolved, @sentry/nextjs unlocks automatic request-context correlation that @sentry/cloudflare standalone doesn't provide.

name: Intake birth-details: 'I don't know my birth time' checkbox z-index
description: Checkbox on intake birth-details page sits on top of time picker / calendar picker inputs — picker is z-index under it, so clicking through to change time/date is blocked or visually obscured. Surfaced 2026-05-24 during gift-claim repro on staging. Fix surface: wrapper around conditional checkbox at birth-details step (search for time_unknown field key in src/components/IntakeForm/).

name: U7 — Studio preview of customer-facing pages
description: Becky can't preview booking / thank-you / listen / /my-gifts from inside Studio. Explore: extending Presentation Tool wiring OR a Sanity Studio plugin.

name: Josephine — edit + preview emails in Studio (Phase 7, paused on branch)
description:
WHAT IT DOES (the feature Josephine sees in Sanity Studio):

Josephine opens any of the 7 customer-facing email singletons in Studio and edits subject / preview / greeting / body / CTA / signoff as plain Sanity fields. The document pane carries a second tab `Preview` (S.view.component(EmailPreview)) that renders a sandboxed iframe of the rendered React Email — live, against draft content (Sanity `previewDrafts` perspective). So she edits → tabs to Preview → sees the email as a customer would, before publishing. Slot validation (`{firstName}`, `{readingName}`, etc.) blocks publish on any unknown token, so a typo can't ship as literal text into a customer inbox.

COVERED TEMPLATES (7):
- emailOrderConfirmation
- emailDay2Started
- emailDay7Delivery
- emailGiftClaim
- emailGiftPurchaseConfirmation
- emailMagicLink
- emailPrivacyExport (new singleton in this branch — replaces the inline-HTML GDPR Art. 20 export)

PIECES (what the branch ships):
- src/lib/emails/slots.ts (+ tests) — canonical EMAIL_ALLOWED_SLOTS + extract/validate/format helpers
- src/lib/emails/PrivacyExport.tsx (+ tests) — React Email template for Art. 20
- src/lib/emails/preview-fixtures.ts — fixture vars + per-template defaults map
- src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx — iframe content endpoint (Origin/Referer allowlist + type validation + draft fetch via SANITY_READ_TOKEN + previewDrafts perspective + dispatch across all 7 templates)
- studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx — Sanity document-pane view (sandboxed iframe pointed at preview route)
- studio/lib/validateSlots.ts — Sanity Rule.custom helper importing the canonical allowlist
- studio/schemas/emailPrivacyExport.ts — new singleton
- studio/schemas/emailOrderConfirmation/Day2/Day7/GiftClaim/GiftPurchase/MagicLink — slot-validation wired on every slot-bearing field
- studio/schemas/deskStructure.ts — emailSingletonListItem helper attaching .views([form, Preview])
- src/middleware.ts — isEmailPreview path-gate for /api/email-preview/*

STATUS (2026-05-23):
- Branch: feat/phase-7-email-sanity-cms, WIP commit f6568e9, on top of main d607ada (27 files, +972/-23).
- Quality gates clean locally: lint, typecheck, vitest (1472 tests), build.
- NOT shipped: seed migration not run on either Sanity dataset, Pentester audit not run, Studio iframe never smoke-tested with a real reviewer, branch never pushed, no PR opened.
- Pause is fully reversible (git branch -D feat/phase-7-email-sanity-cms). PRD has resume instructions: www/MEMORY/WORK/20260517-220000_phase-7-email-sanity-cms/PRD.md § Resume Instructions.

WHY PAUSED:
Main was diverging fast with v1.1.0 hot-fix arc and the U2/U6 listen-loop investigation. Resuming requires rebasing the branch onto current release/v1.2.0 (or main once 1.2.0 merges) — the 27-file diff doesn't conflict with anything load-bearing as of last check, but rebase + re-run tests before opening the PR.

RESUME CHECKLIST: see subtasks (seed migration → Pentester audit → Studio iframe smoke → push + PR).

name: Parallel-safe non-blockers (queued)

name: Apply mode — repair targeted rows
description: Re-points recipient_user_id for each flagged row from the dry-run CSV. Single transaction per submission. Idempotent: re-running on a repaired row no-ops. Invoke as: pnpm tsx scripts/repair-recipient-user-id.mts --apply --csv <path>.

name: Sub-PR D — Data repair script (scripts/repair-recipient-user-id.mts)
description: Repairs bb5fe157 and any other historically corrupted rows by re-pointing recipient_user_id from purchaser (5a72100c) → recipient mgertzen2 (1a87c68d). PRD Phase F. Verification step that closes hold-gate items #10 (U2) and #14 (U6).

name: Plan: heuristic meta-retro — generalize learnings across projects/sessions
description:
Planning task (no execution yet — design the approach first).

Goal: take the raw retrospective output from the parent task and run a *heuristic* pass over it — i.e. not just 'what happened on Josephine' but 'what patterns repeat across sessions/projects and what can we change in the work process itself.'

Inputs:
- SessionHarvester output across this workspace (parent task surfaces it)
- Optionally: SessionHarvester across other project workspaces (~/dev/projects/*) to triangulate
- Existing global feedback memories (~/.claude/projects/*/memory/feedback_*.md)
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md AI Steering Rules — candidate edits land here, not in project CLAUDE.md

Heuristics to apply:
- Which mistakes/frictions repeat across >1 project? (those become global steering rules, not project-local)
- Which decisions, once made, kept getting relitigated? (those become binding-constraint patterns)
- Which workflows produced euphoric surprise vs grinding? (efficiency signal)
- Where did subagent delegation help vs hurt? (process tuning)
- Which memory entries went stale fast? (memory hygiene)

Output of THIS planning task: a short plan doc describing the heuristic method, the output schema (e.g. 'global rules to add', 'project-local rules to add', 'process changes', 'tooling gaps'), and how it differs from the literal project retro in the parent task. Implementation happens after plan is approved.

(2) Add NEXT_PUBLIC_CLARITY_PROJECT_ID to GH repo Variables (no env-scoped override; same project for prod + staging). (3) Trigger production deploy so new build inlines env var. (4) DevTools-verify https://www.clarity.ms/tag/<id> loads without CSP errors. (5) Run set -a && source .env.local && set +a && pnpm tsx scripts/migrate-privacy-clarity.ts against both Sanity datasets (idempotent). (6) In Clarity dashboard, verify masking on real intake-form sessions — DOB / first_name / last_name / photo render as redacted blocks.

name: C3-b — /my-gifts empty listing (purchaser_user_id race at create time)
description: C3 'empty list' half: purchaser_user_id null on some gift rows. src/app/api/booking/gift/route.ts:239 calls getOrCreateUser({email, name}).catch(() => null) — transient user-resolve failure silently produces purchaser_user_id = NULL and the row never surfaces in /my-gifts. Trigger: (a) real customer reports empty /my-gifts after successful purchase, OR (b) routine D1 audit finds purchaser_user_id IS NULL AND is_gift = 1 AND status = 'paid' rows. Investigation: SELECT id, email, purchaser_user_id, recipient_user_id FROM submissions WHERE is_gift = 1 AND status = 'paid' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50. If any null, tighten the getOrCreateUser retry/backfill.

name: CancelScheduledControl component
description: 5s confirm-armed; j-rose tint per Quiet Archivist (NO alarm-red). Copy explicitly mentions 'non-refundable'. Renders cancelled pill on card after success.

name: Verify via sampling
description: Sanity: *[_type == "submission"] | order(_createdAt desc)[0..5] returns zero test entries. D1: SELECT id, email, created_at FROM submissions ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10 returns zero test entries.

name: Merge release/v1.2.0 → main

Merge unblocks main; the prod-D1-migration apply (T1_3) lands at the same window. Sequence: (1) confirm all hold-gate items above closed, (2) gh pr create merge release/v1.2.0 → main, (3) apply D1 migrations 0004→0012 in same window, (4) deploy production, (5) re-walk smoke against apex.

name: POST /api/gifts/[id]/cancel-scheduled route + auth + ordering

name: Phase 7+8 retrospective
description:
Run a retrospective on the recently-completed phases (Phase 7 email Sanity CMS work + Phase 8 pen test / P1 header fixes) and any in-flight learnings.

Method:
- Use `bun ~/.claude/PAI/Tools/SessionHarvester.ts` to scan all sessions in this workspace (cwd: ~/dev/projects/josephine-soul-readings/www) for surfaced patterns, friction, wins, decisions worth canonizing.
- Cross-reference with project_phase8_complete.md memory and recent feedback_*.md memories.
- Output: short retro doc (what worked / what hurt / durable rules to capture) + any new feedback memories or CLAUDE.md edits that should land.

Deliverable: a retro doc + concrete follow-ups landed as new dex tasks or memory entries.

Also sweep maxgertzen+variant prefix rows from 2026-05-24 (incl. submission 67b20b5e-5d91-4dd9-9530-f902f7b3d827 patched for DeliveredSurface verification).

Generate locally: openssl rand -base64 32. Save to password manager BEFORE pasting into wrangler. wrangler secret put ADMIN_API_KEY.

Becky delivery + listen), J2 gift self-send, J3 gift recipient, J4 scheduled gift, J5 /my-gifts (send-now + cancel-scheduled + Studio editability), J7 privacy export, J8 contact, J9 Sanity Live, J10 mobile, J11 admin email checklist. The 'Add' dialog shows username + secret ONCE — copy both immediately. Without these, cascade's Mixpanel data-deletions returns 'mixpanel: not configured' partial-failure and Mixpanel event/profile data is NOT deleted. Run: wrangler secret put MIXPANEL_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_USERNAME + wrangler secret put MIXPANEL_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_SECRET.

name: Apex unpark hold-gate

Closes the path from staging-only operation to real customer traffic. All items must clear before flipping NEXT_PUBLIC_UNDER_CONSTRUCTION = 0 and pasting sk_live_* into the worker.

name: Storybook page-level stories — primary UX/UI review surface
description:
WHY (the win Max named, 2026-05-23):
"See the design and tweak it" is currently expensive — every iteration requires pnpm dev, manually navigating to the page, seeding the right state in D1/Sanity, then hot-reloading. For Becky/Max-driven UX review on pages like /listen, /book, /intake, /letter, /thank-you, /my-gifts, /my-readings, that loop is too slow to actually try variations.

Storybook with mocked Sanity data turns this into: open the story, edit the component file, see the change in <1s with deterministic mock data. Becky doesn't have to be in the loop to provide fixtures.

POSITIONING (locked 2026-05-23):
This is a GATE for page-level UX/UI review work, not launch-blocking on its own. Going forward, "review the UX of page X" defaults to "do it in Storybook with the mocked story for page X." If a page doesn't have a story yet, the first task in any UX-review for that page is to add the story.

Exceptions: the U3/U4/U5/U7/U8 launch-blocking UX punch list (Epic wz9t979j) ships through the live dev server because Storybook setup likely won't beat apex unpark.

SCOPE (locked 2026-05-23):
Pages first, with mocked Sanity data. Components that pages compose get stories implicitly as their pages story. A pure-component-library Phase 2 may come later if Becky wants a design-system catalog, but that's not the immediate win.

Target page set (initial):
- /book/[slug] (entry)
- /letter
- /intake
- /thank-you/[readingId]
- /listen/[id] (incl. all 4 states: cold-visit / check-email / rested / DeliveredSurface)
- /my-gifts (incl. send-now armed, cancel-scheduled armed, claimed, scheduled, paid)
- /my-readings
- /my-readings/[id]

OPEN QUESTIONS (research-gated):
- Storybook version + Next.js App Router + RSC support (9.x latest as of 2026)
- Tailwind v4 + @theme directive wiring into preview-head
- Sanity GROQ mocking strategy: MSW handlers in the browser context (works — workerd-isolate gotcha from CLAUDE.md doesn't apply here), OR Sanity-client mock at the module level, OR prop-level injection of fetched data
- Storybook MCP — confirm canonical one (Storybook team announced an MCP server in 2025), what it exposes, whether it's useful for AI-assisted story authoring
- Routing: how to mock useParams / useSearchParams / next/navigation under @storybook/nextjs
- RSC stories: support landed in 8.4; verify it actually works for our App Router patterns
- Hosting: local-only vs static export deployed somewhere (Cloudflare Pages? GH Pages? Chromatic?) vs visual-regression testing in CI
- Workerd dev-bindings: do they need to be stubbed for stories to load, or does Storybook avoid the worker context entirely?
- Build perf: how Storybook builds against this Next.js codebase + how much it slows the local dev loop

RESUME PATH: research subtask first → plan-discussion subtask reads the research + locks decisions → implementation subtasks get filed AFTER the plan-discussion lands. Implementation tasks are deliberately NOT pre-created — let the research decide what the right shape is.

name: defineLocations additive layer (showHref: false per email type)
description:
ADDITIVE — strictly redundant with the per-document tab, but matches Sanity Studio v5.13+ official 'Email Preview' DocumentLocation example (changelog March 2026 added showHref + icon for non-URL preview targets).

WHAT
1. studio/presentation.ts (currently registers presentationTool with structureTool + visionTool + colorInput per sanity.config.ts:15-25). Add resolve.locations entries for each of the 8 email singleton types.
2. Each entry: { type: "emailOrderConfirmation", ... }, locations: ({ document, perspective }) => ({ locations: [{ title: "Email preview", showHref: false, icon: <EnvelopeIcon /> }] }).
3. No href — showHref: false hides the link affordance (per the v5.13 example).
4. Surfaces emails in Presentation's "Used on" panel; Becky can navigate via Presentation OR via the document tab interchangeably.

WHY MAX KEPT IT (locked 2026-05-24)
Defer-to-Becky's-habits would have been "skip if she navigates direct to documents", but Max chose to add as a ~1h strictly-additive pass. Reasoning: matches Sanity's own published 2026 pattern; if Becky ever drifts to Presentation-first navigation, the surface is ready.

REFERENCE
- Sanity Studio v5.13.0 changelog: sanity.io/docs/changelog/studio-NS4xMi4w
- Presentation resolver API: sanity.io/docs/visual-editing/presentation-resolver-api

OUT OF SCOPE: no real URL routing for emails. defineLocations entries are presentation-tool affordances only, not visual-editing overlays (which are disqualified for table-based React Email per research Topic 3).

name: U3 — Listen-page download filenames (Content-Disposition)
description: Voice-note + PDF downloads arrive with Sanity asset hashes as filenames. Should preserve original Sanity asset filename OR set readable default via Content-Disposition (e.g. soul-blueprint-voice-note.mp3). Fix surface: /api/listen/[id]/audio + /api/listen/[id]/pdf route handlers. Quick-win sub-PR candidate alongside U5.

name: Smoke-walk fix-arc deferrals (C3-b, C4-b, C5)
description: From 2026-05-21 smoke-walk arc (β/δ/α/γ Sub-PR sequence shipped 5 fixes; these 3 didn't ship — each with explicit promotion trigger). Source: www/MEMORY/WORK/20260521-smoke-walk/HANDOFF.md.

name: Promoted UX items — U3, U4, U5, U7, U8 (launch-blocking)
description: Promoted 2026-05-23 from BACKLOG to apex-unpark hold-gate items #11-#16. SESSION_BOOT ship order: U2 (loop, P0, in Epic 2) → U3/U5 quick-wins → U6 (merged into U2) → U4 sweep → U7+U8 Studio bundle.

name: Unit tests — happy + 409 already-fired + 409 already-cancelled
description: Three units covering the WHERE-guard branches.

name: End-to-end cascade smoke after secrets land
description: After ADMIN_API_KEY + Mixpanel pair both set: (1) Studio at withjosephine.sanity.studio/production. (2) Pick test submission doc (cascade is irreversible per Stripe Redaction). (3) 'Delete customer data' → type DELETE → paste admin token → 'Run cascade delete'. (4) Toast should say 'Customer data cascade complete' or list partial-failures (Brevo not-configured expected pre-vetting). (5) deletion_log in production D1 — expect TWO rows per user_id: started + completed with mixpanel_task_id + stripe_redaction_job_id non-null. (6) Mixpanel Project Settings → Data Deletion Requests shows pending/processing entry. (7) Stripe Dashboard → Settings → Privacy → Redaction Jobs shows validating → ready. (8) Re-trigger doc action on same submission → toast 'no recipient user' (idempotency).

name: C4-b — J3 claim/intake heading + listen-page greeting (Sanity schema)
description: Sub-PR δ (#161) closed the two thank-you surfaces. Remaining: (a) studio/schemas/giftIntakePage.ts headingWelcome field has no {recipientName} token → needs token + slot pass. (b) studio/schemas/listenPage.ts has no recipient-greeting field → needs new schema field + prop threading through ListenView. Trigger: next session touching Sanity copy schemas OR Becky asks to personalise either surface.

name: Set RESEND_E2E_DRY_RUN_SECRET on staging worker
description: Optional but recommended for hygiene — sandbox-prefix guard from PR #172 already plugs the leak, so no longer load-bearing. Run: openssl rand -hex 32 | pnpm exec wrangler secret put RESEND_E2E_DRY_RUN_SECRET --env staging. Paste same value into Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → Environments → staging → STAGING_RESEND_E2E_DRY_RUN_SECRET. Both must be identical.

name: Research — Sanity live-preview alternatives for transactional emails (re-evaluate D-1)
description:
PURPOSE
Validate or replace Phase 7 PRD decision D-1 before resuming PR-A1 ship.
D-1 LOCKED IN: custom S.view.component(EmailPreview) tab on each email
singleton, rendering /api/email-preview/[type] in a sandboxed iframe
against draft content via previewDrafts perspective.

D-1 REJECTED: Presentation Tool + defineLocations ("emails aren't web
routes").
D-1 NOT CONSIDERED: Sanity Visual Editing, custom field-level UI input
components, community email-preview plugins, send-to-test-inbox pattern,
Storybook-as-preview hybrid.

DELIVERABLE
Written report at www/MEMORY/WORK/<slug>/RESEARCH.md (gitignored per repo
convention) with vendor-doc citations and a recommendation: either
"Phase 7 D-1 stays — resume PR-A1 as-is" OR "pivot to approach X — rework
PR-A1's preview surface".

TOPICS TO COVER

1. CURRENT D-1 — what's actually shipped on the paused branch
- Read: studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx,
src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx,
src/middleware.ts (CSP widening for frame-ancestors),
studio/schemas/deskStructure.ts
(.views([form, S.view.component(EmailPreview)])).
- Honest assessment: does it actually deliver "easily live preview" for Becky?
- Known gaps (PRD risks 1, 2): CSP frame-ancestors widening, draft auth via
Origin allowlist.

2. SANITY PRESENTATION TOOL — defineLocations applied to emails
- 2026 API state. Can a non-URL doc-type be "located" at
/api/email-preview/[type]?
- Click-to-edit overlays on email body — useful for Becky or visual noise?
- Compare DX (click-through-from-list vs in-document-tab).
- Cost: rewrite of the iframe surface vs swap of the resolver.

3. SANITY VISUAL EDITING (enableVisualEditing) — overlay model
- Same URL constraint as Presentation. Compatible with
/api/email-preview/[type]?
- HTML-overlay UX on an iframed React Email — does it work? Is the email
layout
compatible with the overlay positioning?

4. CUSTOM FIELD-LEVEL UI INPUT COMPONENTS
- Sanity v5 components.input override per-field. Show inline preview NEXT to
subject / body / etc. as Becky edits.
- Granular but more code surface. Editor sees per-field preview, not
whole-email.

5. COMMUNITY SANITY PLUGINS for email preview
- Survey sanity.io/plugins for anything email-specific (e.g. mailto-preview,
react-email-preview, transactional-email integrations).
- Active vs abandoned (last commit date matters).

6. SEND-TO-TEST-INBOX PATTERN
- Studio doc-action: "Send preview to <becky-email>". Real-world rendering
across Gmail / Apple Mail / Outlook.
- Tradeoffs: slowest loop (~10s per send), most accurate (catches
client-specific
rendering bugs).
- Could be ADDITIVE to whichever live-preview approach wins.

7. STORYBOOK-AS-EMAIL-PREVIEW (cross-pollination with the storybook epic)
- Stories for each email template with arg controls for slot values.
- Pro: editor changes content in Storybook UI, sees instant React-Email
render.
- Con: Storybook is for developers, not Sanity editors. Becky doesn't use
Storybook.
- Hybrid: keep Storybook for dev-side email-template iteration; D-1 (or alt)
for
editor-side Sanity-content preview.

8. OFFLINE RENDER-CLI WORKFLOW (existing scripts/render-email-previews.ts)
- Today's tool. Watch-mode + browser-refresh pattern would be cheap to extend.
- Editor-unfriendly (CLI), but zero new code surface.

9. CROSS-CLIENT RENDERING — the dirty secret
- React Email + iframed preview shows Chrome rendering, not
Gmail/Outlook/Apple Mail.
- Email clients strip CSS aggressively (no flexbox-gap, no custom properties
without
fallbacks, no @media in some clients).
- Should D-1 (or alt) integrate Litmus / Email-On-Acid / Resend's built-in
cross-client preview? Cost? Or does the send-to-test pattern fill this gap?

10. SECURITY DELTA vs CURRENT D-1
- D-1 ships CSP frame-ancestors widening + cross-package Studio→worker draft
fetch.
- Each alternative: what's its security surface? Better, worse, neutral?

11. DECISION RUBRIC + RECOMMENDATION
- Decision criteria: (a) editor UX for Becky (most important), (b) code
complexity,
(c) security surface, (d) accuracy of preview vs real email rendering, (e)
code-
already-written cost (D-1 is built; pivot = rewrite).
- Final verdict: stay with D-1, or pivot. If pivot, name the replacement.

CONSTRAINTS

- Treat docs / blog posts from 2024 and earlier with skepticism — Sanity v5 +
the
Presentation Tool stabilization landed late 2024 / early 2025.
- Read the paused branch's actual files (studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx,
src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx, src/middleware.ts CSP changes,
studio/schemas/deskStructure.ts). Don't theorize about D-1 — read the
implementation.
- The branch is `feat/phase-7-email-sanity-cms`, commit `f6568e9`. Check it
out
read-only OR use `git show` to inspect.
- Do NOT propose a plan / new PRD. Output is research — Max + a follow-up
plan-discussion decides the path.

OUTPUT: written report. Lead each topic with the answer. Cite vendor docs and
the paused-branch files inline. End with the recommendation.

STARTING REFERENCE (sent by Max 2026-05-23):
https://www.sanity.io/docs/studio/create-custom-document-views-with-structure-builder

This is the canonical Sanity docs page for the approach D-1 currently uses
(custom document views via S.view.component). Ground topic 1 (current D-1
assessment) in this doc; cross-check against the actual implementation on
the paused branch. If the doc reveals capabilities D-1 isn't using (e.g.
multiple coordinated views, view-level routing, dynamic view loading),
flag them — they may be cheaper improvements than a wholesale pivot.

ADDITIONAL REFERENCE (sent by Max 2026-05-23):
https://github.com/sanity-io/plugins

Official Sanity plugins monorepo — canonical source for maintained-by-Sanity
plugins. Topic 5 (community Sanity plugins for email preview) MUST scan this
repo first (not just sanity.io/plugins search). For each candidate: last
commit date, maintenance posture, Sanity v5 compatibility, fit for our
email-preview use case. If a sanity-io/plugins/* entry covers the gap (e.g.
preview-kit, presentation-related helpers, dashboard widgets that could
host an email preview), call it out explicitly with verdict.
result: ENHANCE D-1, do NOT pivot. Recommendation backed by weighted decision matrix (editor UX, code cost, security, accuracy, sunk-cost). Three actions: (1) swap hand-rolled iframe for sanity-plugin-iframe-pane Iframe with reload.revision=true (~30min if reload.revision works as npmx changelog claims), (2) optional send-to-test doc action (~1d), (3) optional defineLocations additive layer (~1h). Skip Visual Editing (wrong shape for emails). Storybook + render-CLI stay dev-side. 4 open questions logged for plan-discussion: reload.revision validation, send-to-test recipient-allowlist source, defineLocations yes/no, cross-client tooling deferral.

name: Un-gate UA_AUDIT_HASH_DEPLOYED in sandbox CI
description: tests/e2e/specs/listen-roundtrip.spec.ts has test.skip(process.env.UA_AUDIT_HASH_DEPLOYED !== 'true', ...) on the 'link_issued audit row carries user_agent_hash' assertion. Gate added because staging precedes per-PR worker deploys. Now release/v1.2.0 carries Sub-PR A (364ea77) and staging is on new code; gate can come off. Either (a) set UA_AUDIT_HASH_DEPLOYED=true in .github/workflows/e2e-sandbox.yml env, OR (b) remove the test.skip line + helper env-var reference entirely.
